name: Inverted Row
instructions: Set a bar at waist height (e.g., in a Smith machine or power rack). Lie underneath and grab the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder‑width. Extend your legs, keeping your body straight from heels to shoulders. Pull your chest to the bar by driving your elbows back, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Lower back to the start position under control.
form_cues: Keep your body in a straight line — don't let hips sag
Pull with your elbows, not your hands
Squeeze shoulder blades at the top
safety_notes: Make sure the bar is secure and will not roll. If you cannot keep your body straight, bend your knees and plant your feet. This exercise is gentler on the lower back than bent‑over rows.
common_mistakes: Not pulling all the way to the chest
